content:
📜 3. Player-Activated vs. Random Chaos Events

📜 Chaos events can now be triggered by players OR randomly occur.

✔ How This Works:
• Certain chaos events are always random.
• Others can be activated by players if they sacrifice XP, an item, or make a risky move.

✔ Example of Random Chaos Events (No Player Influence):
• The Monkey’s Paw – A seemingly positive event happens but has a hidden cost.
• The Shattered Mirror – A major rule or game mechanic shifts.
• The Forgotten Return – An erased event or character is restored.

✔ Example of Player-Activated Chaos Events (Requires Sacrifice):
• The Great Betrayal → A player sacrifices XP to trigger a faction-wide deception event.
• Rags to Riches → A player spends an item to swap their standing with another.
• The Unwritten Page → A player offers a personal sacrifice to rewrite one rule.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 499 (chunk 499/555)
